title: 20-Minute Veggie Lo Mein Bowl
description: A vibrant and satisfying Asian-inspired noodle dish packed with fresh vegetables and tossed in a savory-sweet sauce. This wholesome lo mein comes together in just 20 minutes, making it perfect for busy weeknight dinners when you crave something both nutritious and delicious.
ingredients: 1/3 cup reduced-sodium tamari (or reduced-sodium soy sauce, or coconut aminos)
1 tablespoon honey (or pure maple syrup to make it vegan)
1 (3.5-ounce) package shiitake mushrooms (stemmed and thinly sliced)
1 red bell pepper (seeded and thinly sliced)
1/4 teaspoon fine sea salt (plus more to taste)
instructions: Cook the noodles: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rapid boil. Cook the 8 ounces spaghetti until just shy of al dente (about 1–2 minutes less than package directions), typically 7 to 10 minutes. Drain and set aside. Reserve about 1/4 cup pasta water in case the finished dish needs loosening.

Make the sauce: While the spaghetti cooks, whisk together the 1/3 cup tamari, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon honey, 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil, and 2 teaspoons sriracha in a small bowl. Set aside.

Cook the firmer vegetables: Heat the 1 tablespoon olive or avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the shiitake mushrooms, red bell pepper, 1 cup grated carrots, and 3 cloves minced garlic. Cook, stirring frequently, until tender, 3 to 4 minutes. If the garlic begins to darken, reduce heat slightly as it can burn quickly at high heat.

Add the quick-cooking vegetables: Add the 3 cups broccoli florets and 1 cup snow peas, toss to combine, and season with the 1/4 teaspoon salt. Cook for another 2 to 3 minutes, until broccoli is bright green and crisp-tender. Don't overcook as the vegetables should retain some bite.

Combine everything: Add the drained noodles and all of the sauce to the skillet. Toss vigorously over heat for 1 to 2 minutes until the sauce coats the noodles evenly and everything is heated through. If the mixture looks dry or sticky, add a splash of reserved pasta water. Taste and adjust salt if desired.
Serve: Divide among 4 bowls and garnish with sliced green onions.
notes: For gluten-free: use gluten-free spaghetti and ensure tamari or coconut aminos are used instead of soy sauce.
For vegan: substitute maple syrup for honey in the sauce.
